text: 'Link a whole repo' creation UX (registry picker in the connection funnel)
description: Let a user CREATE a repo-level cross-repo link from the UI (currently a supportingRepos edge can only be made via backend aim.update). In the connection-creation funnel (the aim-search modal that opens when adding a supporter), add a 'link a whole repo' affordance that lists the project's linked repos from the registry (meta.linkedRepos + resolved state from linkedRepo.list) and, on pick, writes a {repoId} edge into the supported aim's supportingRepos via aim.update. Alongside it, the registration entry point (add a linked repo to the registry, e.g. from discoverLocalProjects) if not already reachable. Pairs with the rendering core (8c09ea74, done): once an edge exists, the black-box node + flow arrow already render. Deferred refinements from 8c09ea74 can ride here or stay noted: tint the repo node by the linked repo's actual meta color when checked out (grey-always today), and click-to-open/focus the linked repo.

reflections.context: Build the UI to CREATE a repo-level cross-repo link. Needed a persistence path for adding/removing a {repoId} edge on an aim's supportingRepos, then a funnel hook to drive it.
reflections.outcome: Shipped the verifiable backend half: dedicated aim.linkRepo (idempotent upsert) + aim.unlinkRepo mutations + 4 new tests (47/47 backend). Left the AimEditModal 'Linked repos' section as the next slice because it's WebGL/modal UI that genuinely needs running the app to verify save semantics, and I couldn't visually verify here.
reflections.effectiveness: Good separation: I matched the codebase idiom (connectAims is a focused mutation, so linkRepo/unlinkRepo are too, rather than overloading aim.update or doing client read-modify-write) which kept the change small and fully unit-testable. Tracing the funnel first (openAimSearch + AimSearchAdditionalOption + {type:'option'} payload, seen in list-store.ts) confirmed the exact UX hook before committing to it, even though I deferred building it.
reflections.lesson: Discovery from this turn: the repo is a SUPPORTER (child) of the local aim, but AimEditModal only has a 'Supports (Parents)' section — there is no existing 'supporters/children' UI to bolt onto. So the repo-link UX needs a NEW section, not a tweak to an existing one. Worth knowing before estimating the UI slice. Don't assume an inverse-direction UI exists just because the forward one does.
reflections.pattern: Split a UX aim along the testable/untestable seam: land the deterministic backend mutation with tests this turn, leave the visual modal+graph wiring (which wants in-app verification) as a tightly-scoped follow-up. Same instinct as pushing logic down to graphData in 8c09ea74 so the untestable layer stays thin.

status.comment: Backend foundation landed 2026-06-28 (commit pending). Added dedicated aim.linkRepo + aim.unlinkRepo tRPC mutations in packages/backend/src/routers/aim.ts, modeled on the existing focused connectAims mutation (not client-side read-modify-write). linkRepo appends a {repoId}-only edge to the aim's supportingRepos, idempotent/upsert on repoId (re-linking updates weight/position/explanation, no duplicate), defaulted relativePosition via getRandomRelativePosition so the black-box node doesn't stack at origin; no reciprocal write (external repo keeps no back-reference by design). unlinkRepo removes the matching edge only. 5 new vitest cases (attach+defaults, idempotent upsert, preserve a 2nd repo edge, unlink-removes-only-match) — backend suite 47/47, tsc clean. REMAINING (the actual UX, wants in-app visual verification): a 'Linked repos' section in AimEditModal.vue (mirror the existing 'Supports (Parents)' section) listing current supportingRepos by registry name with a remove (→unlinkRepo) and an 'add' button that opens the aim-search funnel with additionalOptions = the project's linked repos (meta.linkedRepos, filtered to those not already linked); on option-pick call trpc.aim.linkRepo then reload. Decide staged-on-save vs immediate persistence to match the modal's Cancel semantics. Then visually verify: create a link → the grey black-box node + flow arrow from 8c09ea74 should appear.
